question: Does adding the next-token KL consistency term (tools/train_lora.py --qat-consistency) lower the served-quant BF16-vs-NVFP4 next-token mean-KL and/or raise top-1 agreement versus the QAT-only baseline (olmoe-qat-spark-v3: mean_kl 0.0451, top1 0.906) on the held-out, decontaminated calibration set?
rationale: T6 in agi-proof/gemma4-distillation-research-note.md. The existing QAT path adds a WEIGHT-space quant-push penalty (training/qat.qat_penalty); the consistency term penalises the OUTPUT-space quantity the certifier actually measures (serving/lowram_eval.LowRamGate next-token KL), so it is co-designed with the gate rather than hoping the weight-space penalty transfers.
primaryMetric: BF16-vs-NVFP4 next-token mean-KL on the held-out calib set (lower is better), reported jointly with top-1 agreement
comparison: QAT --qat-consistency arm vs the QAT-only v3 baseline at the SAME base (allenai/OLMoE-1B-7B-0924-Instruct), scheme (nvfp4), epochs, and seed
primaryN: calibration rows >= the v3 set; >= 3 seeds for the train+certify pair

gate.improvementCriterion: the consistency arm must not regress mean_kl or top1 vs v3; a PASS is earned only when top1 >= 0.97 at full quant

decontam: calibration texts decontaminated vs the held-out eval (tools/assert_decontam.py); the calib set must not overlap the certify eval
claimCeiling: candidate_only; canClaimAGI:false. A served-quant PASS may claim ONLY 'served NVFP4 retains BF16 next-token behaviour to the measured KL/top-1 bound' — never 'capability retained' or 'frontier on 8 GB'.
honestLimits: next-token KL/top-1 is a behavioural-fidelity bound, NOT a capability claim; a downstream task eval is a separate experiment
the consistency term costs a 2nd forward/step (eval-mode fp teacher + train-mode quant student)
requires the Spark/RunPod GPU path; offline only proves the KL math (training.qat.offline_invariants, CI-gated via tests/test_track_b.py)

runbook.costGuard: .claude/skills/wisdom-gpu-prebaked/SKILL.md (cheap validation first; confirm zero leaked pods)

enforcement.offline_invariants: training.qat.offline_invariants (next_token_kl_ref: zero on equal, positive on diff) — CI via tests/test_track_b.py::test_qat_offline_invariants

outcomeNote: A NO-GO (consistency term does not move top1 past 0.97) is a valid publishable outcome — log the measured mean_kl/top1 vs v3, do not tune the lambda to the gate.
